topIssues.title: @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 crashes at boot under Bun via engine.io + debug interop
topIssues.author: ayushgupta0610
topIssues.body:
## Summary

After working around #8000 by installing `@elizaos/cli` directly (`bun add -g @elizaos/cli`), the CLI still crashes at boot because `engine.io` (transitive) calls `(0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport")` and `debug_1.default` is `undefined` under Bun's CJS resolver. The boot path eagerly loads `engine.io` even for commands that do not need a server (`create`, `--version`, etc).

## Repro

```bash
bun add -g @elizaos/cli      # installs @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (npm latest)
elizaos create my-app --template project
```

```
3 | exports.Transport = void 0;
4 | const events_1 = require("events");
5 | const parser_v4 = require("engine.io-parser");
6 | const parser_v3 = require("./parser-v3/index");
7 | const debug_1 = require("debug");
8 | const debug = (0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport");
                       ^
TypeError: (0, debug_1.default) is not a function. (In '(0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport")',
'(0, debug_1.default)' is undefined)
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transport.js:8:27
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transports/polling.js:3:1
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transports/index.js:2:1
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/server.js:431:5
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/engine.io.js:6:1

Bun v1.3.10 (macOS arm64)
```

## Root cause

`engine.io`'s compiled `transport.js` emits:

```js
const debug_1 = require("debug");
const debug = (0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport");
```

It assumes a `.default` property on the `debug` CJS module. `debug@4.x` exports the factory directly (`module.exports = createDebug`); there is no `.default` property in the CJS shape. Node's CJS-from-ESM interop synthesizes a `default` property automatically; Bun's resolver does not produce the same shape for this dual-package module under the global-bin code path. This is a known engine.io + Bun interop class, not an elizaOS source-level bug.

## Why this is elizaOS's problem in practice

`@elizaos/cli@1.7.2` eagerly loads `engine.io` / Socket.IO at module-init time of *any* command — even `--help` and `create` walk through `engine.io/build/engine.io.js` → `server.js` → `transports/index.js` → `transport.js` before the command's own logic runs. Commands that don't need a websocket server should not be loading the websocket server stack at parse time.

## Environment

- @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (`npm view @elizaos/cli dist-tags.latest`)
- Node v24.13.0
- Bun v1.3.10
- macOS arm64

## Suggested mitigations (any one would unblock)

1. **Lazy-load `engine.io` / Socket.IO off the boot path.** Move the server imports behind the commands that actually serve (`start`, `dev`). `create`, `info`, `upgrade`, `--version`, `--help` should not transitively touch `engine.io`. This is also a startup-latency win independent of the Bun bug.
2. **Pin / patch the transitive dep.** Either pin `engine.io` to a build whose compiled output uses `__importDefault`, or pin `debug` to a version that exports a named `default` property.
3. **Document Bun support explicitly.** If Bun is a supported runtime for the global CLI, gate publish on a `bun`-runtime smoke (see #8000). If not, the install instructions should say so.

## Status on develop

Verified: develop's `packages/elizaos@2.0.3` scaffolds a project end-to-end via Node (`node packages/elizaos/dist/cli.js create … --template project --yes` succeeds against a fresh `bun install` of the workspace). Either the boot was trimmed or the dep tree was restructured — would be useful for maintainers to surface what changed, since that change is the basis for closing this on the next stable release.

## Workaround

Same as #8000 — use the develop CLI directly until a release supersedes 1.7.2 on `latest`.

topIssues.title: elizaos@1.7.2 global install crashes with ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED
topIssues.author: ayushgupta0610
topIssues.body:
## Summary

The current `npm dist-tag latest` for both `elizaos` and `@elizaos/cli` is `1.7.2`. A clean global install fails on the very first command because the `elizaos` alias package's bin shim imports a subpath that `@elizaos/cli@1.7.2`'s `exports` map does not whitelist.

## Repro

```bash
bun add -g elizaos        # or: npm i -g elizaos
elizaos --version         # any subcommand reproduces
```

```
Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './dist/index.js' is not defined by "exports" in
/Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@elizaos/cli/package.json
imported from /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/elizaos/bin/elizaos.js
    at exportsNotFound (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:313:10)
    at packageExportsResolve (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:661:9)
    ...
  code: 'ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED'
```

## Root cause (verified from npm registry)

`elizaos@1.7.2` ships `bin/elizaos.js`:

```js
import '@elizaos/cli/dist/index.js';
```

`@elizaos/cli@1.7.2` declares:

```json
"exports": {
  ".": "./dist/index.js",
  "./package.json": "./package.json"
}
```

Node's ESM resolver enforces `exports` strictly — `./dist/index.js` as a subpath is blocked even though the file exists. The shim should import the package root (`'@elizaos/cli'`), not a subpath.

## Environment

- elizaos@1.7.2 (`latest` tag)
- @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (`latest` tag)
- Node v24.13.0
- Bun v1.3.10
- macOS arm64

Confirmed from npm registry:

```
$ npm view elizaos dist-tags
{ latest: '1.7.2', next: '2.0.0-alpha.32', alpha: '2.0.0-alpha.535', beta: '2.0.0-beta.5' }

$ npm view @elizaos/cli dist-tags
{ beta: '1.7.2-beta.0', latest: '1.7.2', alpha: '1.7.3-alpha.4' }
```

## Status on develop

Develop has already restructured: `packages/elizaos` is the CLI itself at v2.0.3; no alias shim, no inter-package subpath import. `@elizaos/cli@1.7.3-alpha.4` also no longer has a separate shim — its `bin` points directly to `dist/index.js`. The structural fix is in; the gap is that no release has superseded the broken `latest` tag.

## What would prevent regression

A pre-publish global-install smoke test:

1. `npm pack` → tarball
2. `npm i -g <tarball>` into a sandboxed prefix
3. Run `<prefix>/bin/elizaos --version` under **both** Node and Bun
4. Assert exit code 0

The existing `packages/elizaos/scripts/packaged-smoke.mjs` does a local install but not a global one — global install resolves bin via a different path that is stricter on `exports`. Happy to send a PR.

Optional bonus: a static linter that walks `packages/*/package.json`, finds any `bin`-listed file doing `import 'foo/sub/path.js'`, and verifies `foo`'s `exports` map permits that subpath. Would catch this entire class of bug pre-publish.

## Workaround for users hitting this today

```bash
# Use the develop branch CLI directly — confirmed working:
git clone https://github.com/elizaos/eliza.git && cd eliza
bun install
bun run --cwd packages/elizaos build
node packages/elizaos/dist/cli.js create my-app --template project --yes

# Or pin a pre-release tag:
npm i -g elizaos@beta
```

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): wire the child→parent USE_SKILL parent-agent broker dispatcher
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## What

Closes the production gap that left the capped self-spend autonomy mechanism (merged in #8250) unreachable by a live agent. A `/economics` sub-agent can now autonomously drive the monetized-app loop through the parent-agent Cloud command broker.

## Why

`runParentAgentBroker` / `runCloudCommand` (the only emitter of `spend_auto_authorized`) had **no production caller**: nothing parsed a child's `USE_SKILL parent-agent {…}` request, `buildSkillsManifest({ virtualSkills })` was never called, and the autonomy was only proven via Vitest. See `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/docs/economics-goal-runbook.md` (bundled/updated here).

## How (the three gaps, closed)

1. **Dispatch** — new `parent-agent-dispatch.ts`: a string/escape-aware extractor for `USE_SKILL parent-agent {json}` (handles streaming/partial JSON, braces inside strings, nested params, markdown backticks, malformed markers) + a dispatch helper that runs the directive through `runParentAgentBroker` and streams `result.text` back via `acp.sendToSession`. Wired into `SubAgentRouter.handleEvent`: accumulates the child's `message` chunks, dispatches the first complete directive, caps round-trips via `ACPX_SUB_AGENT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP`. **Marker-guarded** — only acts on text containing `USE_SKILL parent-agent`, which ordinary coding tasks never emit, so the session-event hot path is inert for every other flow.
2. **Advertise** — `spawnAgentForTask` writes a `SKILLS.md` for `capabilityProfile === "economics"` tasks via `buildSkillsManifest(..., { recommendedSlugs: ["build-monetized-app","eliza-cloud"], virtualSkills: [PARENT_AGENT_BROKER_MANIFEST_ENTRY] })` so the child learns the slug + arg contract. Best-effort (never fatal).
3. **Estimate** — the broker's unknown-cost stall now returns an *actionable* instruction (fetch a quote with `domains.check`, retry with `params.spendEstimateUsd`) instead of a human-only yes/no, and the manifest guidance advertises the same pattern — so `domains.buy` self-authorizes within the cap without a human turn.

## Verification

- New `parent-agent-dispatch.test.ts` (11 cases): extractor edge cases + the broker→`sendToSession` bridge end to end via the network-free `list-cloud-commands` mode.
- Existing `parent-agent-broker.test.ts` + `skill-manifest.test.ts` still green (no regression from the preview/guidance change).
- Plugin typecheck clean; biome clean.
- End-to-end (a real `spend_auto_authorized` on `domains.buy`/`containers.create`) still needs a live ACP backend — the runbook documents the exact steps.

## Safety

The hot-path hook is a no-op unless the child emits the literal `USE_SKILL parent-agent` marker. Paid/mutating commands remain gated by the existing capped spend allowance (`ELIZA_AGENT_SPEND_CAP_USD`, default 0 = confirm everything); destructive commands always require a human.

topPRs.title: fix(orchestrator): forward Windows env (Path casing) to ACP sub-agents
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## What

`shouldForwardEnv` (the allowlist `buildEnv` uses to decide which host env vars a
spawned ACP coding sub-agent inherits) matched the OS search path as
`key === "PATH"` — case-sensitively.

The repo runtime is Bun, and **Bun on Windows reports the key as `Path`, not
`PATH`**. So `buildEnv` forwarded *none* of it, and every spawned ACP sub-agent
inherited an empty `PATH`. The vendored opencode shim then died at spawn with:

```
'bun' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.
```

i.e. ACP-driven coding agents were unusable on Windows.

## Fix

- Match the OS system vars **case-insensitively** (a `Set` keyed by uppercase
  name) and broaden the set to the Windows essentials a spawned shell + Bun +
  the agent's config/cache resolution need (`PATHEXT`, `SYSTEMROOT`, `COMSPEC`,
  `SYSTEMDRIVE`, `TEMP`/`TMP`, `USERPROFILE`, `APPDATA`/`LOCALAPPDATA`,
  `PROGRAMFILES`, …) alongside the existing POSIX names.
- **Canonicalize** each forwarded system var to its uppercase form in `buildEnv`
  (all three sources: `process.env`, `customCredentials`, `extra`) so a child
  never inherits two casings of the same var (the winner is undefined on
  Windows) and JS consumers that read `env.PATH` case-sensitively resolve it.
- Extract the projection into the pure, exported `forwardableSubAgentEnv` /
  `canonicalForwardedEnvKey` so the behavior is unit-testable.

The deny-list (`isDeniedSubAgentEnvKey`) still runs **first** (deny wins over
allow), so no secret is newly forwarded — covered by the existing + new tests.

## Testing

- `__tests__/unit/should-forward-env.test.ts` — added Windows-casing,
  no-over-match, system-var, canonicalization, and dual-casing-collapse cases
  (17 pass).
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator typecheck` clean.

## How it was surfaced

Running the monetized-app economics `/goal` loop end-to-end on Windows: a real
`opencode` ACP sub-agent (Cerebras `gpt-oss-120b`) would not spawn until the
PATH was forwarded.

topPRs.title: chore(lifeops): sweep up post-decomposition leftovers
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## Background

Follow-up to #8314 — that PR removed the runtime crash (`registerLifeOpsApp is not a function` white-screen) by dropping the calls and the two stale ambient `.d.ts` shims in `packages/app`. Verify agent then flagged a long tail of now-dead references the hotfix didn't sweep: stub modules, test fixtures, view-coverage tests, env-alias scripts, CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md docs, and a couple of narrative slop comments. This PR removes them in one pass so the plugin-lifeops → plugin-personal-assistant decomposition is actually finished.

## What

- **packages/agent tests** — drop dead `plugin-personal-assistant` entries from `TUI_CAPABILITY_SOURCE_MAP`, `PLUGIN_VIEW_MANIFESTS`, `PLUGIN_BUNDLES`, `CONVERTED_PLUGINS`, `ALL_XR_VIEW_IDS`, `xrComponentSrc`. PA no longer ships any view post-decomposition.
- **packages/agent runtime/eliza.ts** — rewrite the module-cycle sentinel comment to reference the current `@elizaos/plugin-personal-assistant` + `@elizaos/plugin-companion` specs instead of the long-gone `@elizaos/app-lifeops` / `@elizaos/app-companion`.
- **packages/app**
  - Delete `src/plugin-loader.ts` outright — no importer, and it carried 21 hallucinated `@elizaos/app-*` package specs that don't exist.
  - Trim `src/main.tsx`: drop the commit-archeology preamble + the `registerLifeOpsApp() no longer exists` narrative slop left over from the #8314 fix.
  - Drop the lifeops `LEVELS` entry from `test/hmr/hmr-dependency-levels.spec.ts` (file no longer exists).
  - Drop `LifeOps*` exports from `test/brand-surface.test.ts` file list.
  - Sync `AGENTS.md` + `CLAUDE.md` to the post-decomposition reality: drop the `registerLifeOpsApp` reference, drop the Views section claiming `dist/views/bundle.js`, drop the `build:views` script row.
- **packages/elizaos templates** — drop `LifeOps*` exports from `optional-eliza-app-stub.tsx`; delete the dead `type-stubs/eliza-apps/lifeops-ui.d.ts` stub.
- **packages/scripts/lib/sync-eliza-env-aliases.mjs** — drop the `@elizaos/app-lifeops/register-routes` spec; that package doesn't exist anymore.
- **packages/ui** — rename `plugin-lifeops` → `plugin-personal-assistant` in the `AppsSidebar.stories.tsx` fixture.
- **plugins/plugin-personal-assistant**
  - Sync `AGENTS.md` + `CLAUDE.md` to current shape.
  - Replace the 21-line narrative preamble on `src/ui.ts` with a one-liner.
- **plugins/plugin-xr tests** — update `xr-functional-parity` summary from 24 → 23 plugins; drop PA entries from `xr-bundle-coverage` + `xr-feature-parity`.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run typecheck` (packages/app) — no new errors in changed files; same pre-existing set as develop, line numbers shift only due to deleted comments.
- [x] `vitest plugin-tui-view-coverage / view-bundle-lifecycle / view-agent-surface-coverage / xr-functional-parity / xr-feature-parity / brand-surface` — all pass.
- [x] `xr-bundle-coverage` still has pre-existing failures for plugins whose `dist/views/bundle.js` isn't built; this change actually drops one of those failures (PA).
- [ ] SPA boots cleanly post-merge; no fallout from the plugin-loader.ts deletion (verified no importers exist).

topPRs.title: fix(cloud-frontend): VITE_ENVIRONMENT passthrough for env-gated agent flavors
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## Why

Previous attempt (rejected by workflow adversarial verify) had AGENT_FLAVORS filter that always returned 'unknown' because Vite wipes process.env in the SPA bundle. The env-gated filter never fired and the prod dropdown still showed develop.

## What

- Add `VITE_ENVIRONMENT` to `PUBLIC_ENV_KEYS` in `packages/cloud-frontend/vite.config.ts` so Vite's `define` block + `loadEnv` injects it
- `resolveEnvMode` reads `import.meta.env.VITE_ENVIRONMENT` first, then `process.env.ENVIRONMENT` / `NODE_ENV` fallback (for Worker/Node)
- Replace `AGENT_FLAVORS` superset export with `getAgentFlavorsForEnv()` function
- Update sole consumer (`create-eliza-agent-dialog.tsx`)
- GHA cloud-cf-deploy already exports `VITE_ENVIRONMENT=staging|production` in the build env per branch

## Test plan

- [x] typecheck cloud-shared + cloud-frontend
- [ ] CI green
- [ ] After deploy: staging dashboard 'New Agent' modal shows only develop + custom (not stable)
- [ ] Prod dashboard shows only stable + custom
